programmes.show_description: This episode of the award-winning show, filmed on Red Nose Day, focuses on patients and staff at King's reflecting on the importance of looking on the brighter side of life.
Brand new series: At the Royal Melbourne Hospital, a motorcyclist can't feel his legs after a crash. And a student evacuated out of the Middle East has devastating shrapnel wounds.
Stacie, 38, has fallen out of a tree onto her head. Ashleigh, who's 19, is rushed in after being involved in a high-speed, head-on car crash. And Peter, 78, has abdominal pains.
This episode of the award-winning show, filmed on Red Nose Day, focuses on patients and staff at King's reflecting on the importance of looking on the brighter side of life.
The campers pitch up at at the foot of Scotland's most famous mountain at Ben Nevis Holiday Park, where they take aim at a five-star rating with axe throwing and mountain biking.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Simon O'Brien's restorer Gemma turns an old TV cabinet into a luxury dog bed, while Henry Cole trades in his lifelong love of mechanicals to upcycle wooden furniture.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Oxford, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien get hands-on in a second-hand goods shop. They discover a servant's call bell, and a mysterious pedal-powered tool catches Simon's eye.
The week kicks off at the Juniperlea Inn in Midlothian, where host Carly aims to wow her rivals with her stylish décor. But one guest certainly isn't wowed by the roadside location.
The guests head to Starshinezzz in County Durham. Host Dave Taylor hopes that his renowned hospitality will make him a winner, but a chaotic breakfast could scupper his plans.
It's Barney's turn to host, at The Hideaway at Baxby Manor in North Yorkshire. His guests are charmed by the countryside 'Hobbit Huts', but hidden costs ruin things for one pair.
The last visit of the week is to Jodie and Enya's Hideout Hotel in Hull. The guests are impressed by the self-catered apartments, until a dramatic breakfast derails one pair's stay.
It's payment day, and the B&Bers debate their differences on everything from sleep issues to bathroom hygiene, before the winning B&B is crowned.
Blackpool and Preston. On the first night, student and bartender Rachel's American diner-themed menu confuses her guests. But they all agree that her hosting rocks. (Ep1/5)
Blackpool and Preston. Lady of leisure Jill, who's dined at The Ivy three times, plans to impress with her Italian menu and language skills. And Bernard has the group in hysterics. (Ep2/5)
Blackpool and Preston. Flight attendant Mark plans to give his guests a taste of the high life with duck, venison and gin jelly cake. (Ep3/5)
Blackpool and Preston. Parole officer Bernard serves up a combination of Northern Soul and tasty grub. But there's a walk-out during the main course. (Ep4/5)
Blackpool and Preston. Health assistant Lisa is banking on her no-faff menu to bag her the prize. But tempers flare as some home truths are delivered. (Ep5/5)
This time, the competition begins at Woodleys Farmhouse in Woburn Sands, Buckinghamshire, where newbie B&Ber Toni Kupitz is nervous about her breakfast service.
The B&Bers head to Finch Cottage in Wiltshire, run by husband and wife Eddie and Karen Deighton. But the lack of TVs in the bedrooms is a big turn-off for some of their guests.
Ian and his fiancée Karen host at The Bell Inn in Thorpe-le-Soken, Essex. Their guests are reeled in by a fun day of fishing, but a room with a spooky view gives Frankie a bedtime fright.
The last visit of the week is to Frasers Guest House in Battlesbridge, where sisters Georgie and Frankie hope that their B&B's location in an antiques district will be a hit.
The B&Bers are keen to challenge each other about their feedback on payment day. Accusations of game playing ensure a tense end to the competition, before the winner is announced.
Kirstie and Phil house-hunt for two couples in Heaton and the Tyne Valley. Tom and Liz are fed up with renting, while Michael and Sorrel have just months to find a home.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: In Warwickshire, Gill and John can't decide whether to love their inherited two-bed on the edge of the countryside, or list it and head for the city...
...Clive and Kids: Builder Richard grapples with ceiling beams, while the new pet goats are causing chaos. And for the first time in over a year, Anty John's is scaffolding free.
At St George's in south west London, the trauma team fight to save the life of a young woman after a motorbike accident. And Luke, who's 11, is brought in after having a seizure.
The neurosurgical team at St George's treat a 19-year-old who's sustained multiple spinal injuries in a motocross crash, while two other men are treated for cycling injuries.
Heli-medics rush to help a 15-year-old who's fallen 20 feet after a large kite dragged him off his feet. A collision in Leicester leaves a woman trapped in her car.
At St George's in south west London, the trauma team fight to save the life of a young woman after a motorbike accident. And Luke, who's 11, is brought in after having a seizure.
The neurosurgical team at St George's treat a 19-year-old who's sustained multiple spinal injuries in a motocross crash, while two other men are treated for cycling injuries.
At Dalraddy Holiday Park on a traditional Scottish estate at Aviemore, the campers take a train ride, enjoy some quirky Highland games and battle it out on the paintball field.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien get revved up in Pembrokeshire. They explore mechanic James's sheds, crammed with classic cars, motorbikes and tractors, including a 1928 Model A Ford.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Suffolk to meet pals John and Jane, who've been collecting and swapping items for many years.
For the week's second visit, the B&Bers go glamping at Driftwood Luxury Lodges in Derbyshire. Host Carl is obsessed with interior design, but is his stylish homemade furniture practical?
At the Grantham Arms in Boroughbridge, North Yorkshire, hosts Simon and Joe hope that dazzling décor and Joe's amazing hosting skills will be a winning combination.
Rick and Sarah Blaney host the third visit at The Stamford in Southport. But the seaside stay gets off to a bad start when the 'impersonal' self check-in leaves some guests out in the cold.
Mum and daughter Annelies and Claudia Richardson host the final visit of the week at The Fauconberg Arms in North Yorkshire, where they think the food is just as important as the rooms.
Payment day brings frank discussions about everything from questionable hosting skills to substandard breakfasts, before the B&Bers learn how much they've been paid and who's won.
Birmingham. Self-proclaimed domestic goddess Lisa plans a menu straight from her Malaysian roots, albeit with a few shortcuts... (Ep1/5)
Birmingham. Enigmatic shopping channel producer Martha hopes that her vegan feast will educate - as well as entertain - her guests. But how will the lack of meat go down? (Ep2/5)
Birmingham. On night three, 46-year-old Mark plans a menu of classic comfort food. The smitten host is keen to impress guest Martha but forgets a key ingredient. (Ep3/5)
Birmingham. Youth worker Sonia serves up a selection of restaurant favourites - but are they restaurant quality? (Ep4/5)
Birmingham. Aquarium designer Akil hopes to make a splash on the final day with a homemade seafood tempura starter and sea bass and scallops for the main course. (Ep5/5)
The competition begins at NightSky Glamping in Abersoch, run by Angie and her daughter Caitlin. The guests love the hot tubs and views, but one of them admits that they hate camping.
Visit two is to Johnny Dough's Restaurant with Rooms in Conwy, North Wales. After a boat trip, host Morgan reveals his surprising past. Will his efforts produce good scores in the feedback?
We're off to the Peak District for the week's third visit, at The Royal Oak, run by Kadir. But two sets of guests experience some mouldy misery in their bathrooms.
The last visit of the week is to The Dovecote Inn in Nottinghamshire, run by Sam and Sophie. Can their cracking continental turn things around after an underwhelming day out?
On payment day, cutlery quibbles, grout gripes and breakfast bombshells are debated, before the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.
Kirstie helps a family who are swapping Switzerland for a new home in Hove. But they have a long wish list. Phil is with a couple retiring from Lancashire to the South Downs.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or
programmes.show_description: List It: Can Kirstie breathe new life into Carl and Katy's draughty, inefficient Victorian semi with a roof-raising renovation? Or will they be persuaded by pastures new?
High-end drones explore Britain's country estates. At Penshurst Place in Kent, the Sidney family look to the future, as gardener Tony works across the seasons. (Ep3/4)
Paul, who's 38, is rushed into resus after falling 25 feet from a tree. And Anthony, who was recently diagnosed with Parkinson's and prostate cancer, has fallen down some stairs.
